name: Cabeçó d'Or and Bonalba Sierras Loop
name_original: Sierras de Bonalba y Cabeçò d'Or — Circular por el Camí del Collado de Relleu
description: This difficult 33.3-mile mountain biking route in Sierras de Bonalba y Cabeçò d'Or features 3731 feet of elevation gain and scenic views.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Mutxamel, located in Spain's Alicante province, offers diverse landscapes for outdoor enthusiasts. The region features a mix of rugged mountains, rolling hills, and coastal plains, providing varied terrain for mountain bike trails. Limestone formations, ravines like Barranc del Cocons, and areas with endemic flora characterize the natural environment. This extensive network of trails caters to various skill levels, from easy rides to challenging paths. Best mountain bike trails around Mutxamel The most popular mountain bike trail is Sierras de Bonalba y Cabeçò d'Or — Circular por el Camí del Collado de Relleu, a 33.3 miles (53.5 km) trail that takes 5 hours 2 minutes to complete. This difficult route traverses mountainous terrain with significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Montnegre River Waterfall – Bonalba Trail loop from L'Almaixada, a difficult 36.6 miles (59.4 km) path. This trail offers a dynamic experience through varied Mediterranean landscapes, including river areas and challenging ascents. Local mountain bikers also love the Maigmó Greenway – Long Tunnel loop from Mutxamel, a 39.8 miles (64.5 km) trail leading through diverse scenery, often completed in about 4 hours 7 minutes. This difficult route includes sections of greenway and varied natural features. Mountain biking around Mutxamel is defined by rugged mountains, rolling hills, and coastal plains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easier paths to more challenging routes with significant elevation changes. The routes in Mutxamel are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Mutxamel's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there around Mutxamel?
A: Mutxamel offers a good selection of mountain bike trails, with over 25 routes available on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy rides to challenging paths.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Mutxamel?
A: The terrain around Mutxamel is diverse, featuring rugged mountains, rolling hills, and coastal plains. You'll encounter limestone formations, ravines like Barranc del Cocons, and areas rich in endemic flora, providing a dynamic and engaging experience.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Mutxamel?
A: Yes, Mutxamel has several options for less experienced riders. Out of the 26 available routes, 4 are classified as easy, making them suitable for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Mutxamel is well-suited for experienced mountain bikers, with 13 routes classified as difficult. These trails often feature significant elevation changes and technical sections. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39546507" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Orgegia Forest Park – Monte Oregia Viewpoint loop from Mutxamel&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 28.0 km route.

Q: What are some scenic mountain bike routes in Mutxamel?
A: Many routes offer stunning views of the diverse Mediterranean landscapes. For a route with varied scenery, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39541371" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Puig Campana loop from Mutxamel&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 47.5 km trail that provides vistas of the iconic Puig Campana.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Mutxamel?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails around Mutxamel are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e930138393/sierras-de-bonalba-y-cabeco-d-or-circular-por-el-cami-del-collado-de-relleu" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierras de Bonalba y Cabeçò d'Or — Circular por el Camí del Collado de Relleu&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 53.5 km circular route traversing mountainous terrain.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Mutxamel?
A: Mutxamel's trails often pass by significant natural features. You might encounter areas like El Bec de l'Àguila, a mountainous Special Protection Area for Birds, or the historic Mutxamel and Sant Joan waterwheels near the River Seco. For specific viewpoints, consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2652282" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra Grossa&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2835932" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maigmó Summit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any waterfalls along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the region features natural water elements. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1387987" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montnegre River Waterfall – Bonalba Trail loop from L'Almaixada&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 59.4 km route that specifically highlights a river waterfall, offering a dynamic experience through varied Mediterranean landscapes.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Mutxamel?
A: The mountain bike trails in Mutxamel are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain and the engaging experience offered by the diverse Mediterranean landscapes.

Q: Are there any specific canyons or gorges accessible near the mountain biking routes?
A: Yes, the area around Mutxamel is known for its impressive geological formations. You can explore highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6324366" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco de Aigües Baixes&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5415961" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Les Salines ravine&lt;/a&gt;, which offer dramatic scenery and unique natural beauty.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike trail available in Mutxamel?
A: One of the longer routes you can tackle is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39546529" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maigmó Greenway – Long Tunnel loop from Mutxamel&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult trail spans approximately 64.1 km (39.8 miles) and leads through diverse scenery, often completed in about 4 hours and 7 minutes.
tips: Picturesque path through the Collado del Relleu with spectacular views of the Cabezo de Oro and all the surrounding mountains. A simply wonderful journey

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: This is a difficult mountain biking trail, requiring a high level of fitness and advanced riding skills. Komoot classifies it as 'hard', which often means more than 5 hours of riding or over 3,000 feet of elevation gain, with some sections potentially requiring you to push your bike. It corresponds to STS S3 - S6 technical ratings.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is not suitable for beginners. Its 'difficult' classification indicates that riders need very good fitness and advanced riding skills to navigate the challenging terrain.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Sierras de Bonalba y Cabeçò d'Or mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is demanding and technical, typical of the Alicante region. Expect rocky sections, loose gravel, and potentially loamy patches. Some parts can be 'super chunky' with 'chutes, rock gardens and some scary slab rolls', making it difficult to maintain speed due to its rugged nature.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: The starting point of the route is conveniently located next to a parking lot, making access straightforward for riders arriving by car.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers spectacular views, particularly through the Old Path of Collado de Relleu. You'll enjoy picturesque vistas of the Cabeçò d'Or, the surrounding mountains, and potentially stunning panoramas overlooking the Mediterranean Sea and towns like Benidorm and Calpe.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 53.6 kilometers long with over 1,100 meters of elevation gain. It typically takes around 5 hours to complete for experienced mountain bikers.

faqs.question: Is this a circular mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Sierras de Bonalba y Cabeçò d'Or — Circular por el Camí del Collado de Relleu is a circular route, meaning you will finish at your starting point.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this trail is generally during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers in the Alicante region can be very hot, making the demanding ride more challenging. Winters are usually mild but can bring occasional rain.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this exact trail are not detailed, generally, dogs are permitted on trails in natural areas of Spain, often requiring them to be on a leash. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Cabeçó d'Or i la Grana park?
faqs.answer: There is typically no entrance fee or permit required to access the natural areas like Cabeçó d'Or i la Grana for recreational activities such as mountain biking. However, always respect local regulations and signage regarding conservation and access.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Cabeçó d'Or, El Camí IT5TR2 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana, Camí de la Torre de les Maçanes, Pujada al Castell, and senda Canal de l'Homenet.

waypoints.name: Old Path of Collado de Relleu
